content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 29, 1988


Dan Colegrove
138 Buckeye Street
Woodland, CA 95695

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-88-364

Dear Mr. Colegrove:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Assemblyman Gary
Condit concerning his duties under the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1/  This letter confirms our telephone conversation
of September 13, 1988.

QUESTION

     Assemblyman Condit recently received through the mail an
envelope containing a note and a $50 cash contribution.  The
sender was not identified on the note or envelope.

     If Assemblyman Condit does not want to accept the anonymous
cash contribution, how should he dispose of it?

CONCLUSION

     If Assemblyman Condit does not want to accept the anonymous
cash contribution, he may promptly pay it to the Secretary of
State for deposit in the State General Fund.

ANALYSIS

     Section 84304 prohibits anonymous contributions of $100 or
more.  Assemblyman Condit has received an anonymous


_____________________

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Dan Colegrove
September 29, 1988
Page 2


contribution of $50.2/ Although the $50 anonymous contribution
is not prohibited, Assemblyman Condit prefers not to accept it.

Section 84304 requires the recipient of an anonymous
contribution of $100 or more to promptly pay the contribution
to the Secretary of State for deposit in the State General
Fund. Again, this requirement does not apply to the situation
in question because the anonymous contribution was less than
$100. We suggest, however, that the procedure mandated by
Section 85304 is an appropriate one for Assemblyman Condit to
follow in order to dispose of the anonymous contribution.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[illegible]
By: Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:KED:plh

_____________

2/ In our initial telephone conversation on September 12,
1988, I advised you that it appeared that Assemblyman Condit
had received a $50 gift. However, the note included with the
$50 payment indicates that the payment was made for political
purpose. Therefore, on September 13, 1988, I retracted my
previous advice and informed you Assemblyman Condit has
received a $50 contribution. (Section 82015; Regulation 18215
(copy enclosed).)

88.3104

JEF[]J O SU 94T 08

September 12, 1988
Sacramento, California

Cathy Donovan
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Donovan

This is to confirm our conversation of this afternoon. As you will recall,
I called to ask advice on how to handle a certain situation.

In Assemblyman Condits mail this morning was a small envelope with no name or
return address. Inside was a handwritten note (enclosed) that read, "At 88 yrs.
old I'm too old to meddle much in the Government - but I like what you are doing
Hope this helps to put your fist in Willies big mouth." Stapled to this note were
5 $10 bills.

As we have no way of knowing who sent this, we are unable to return it. Assemblyman
Condit decided to donate this to charity. I called the FPPC to ask the best way
to do this and report it. When you returned my call at approximately 4:30 pm,
you indicated that since there is nothing in the note to indicate that this is
a campaign donation, it should be treated as one treated as a gift. You told me that
a gift can be donated to charity within 30 days upon receipt. You also advised
me to have a notation included on Assemblyman Condit's next Statement of Economic
Interest that on September 12, 1988, this money was received and to give the charity
name and the date they were sent this donation. Finally, you told me that this
would not need to be listed on Assemblyman Condit's Consolidated Campaign Statement
to be filed with the Secretary of State.

Thank you for your advice on this situation. If you have any further questions or
suggestions you may contact me at (916) 445-8570.

Sincerely,

DAN COLEGROVE

at 88 years old I'm too old
to middle much in the
Government - but I like
it that you are doing
it. I hope they'll feed
your horse outside the
mouth

Assemblymans [illegible]
Sacramento, Ca
95-9?

September 20, 1988
Sacramento, CA

Ms. Constance Bird, Treasurer
Citizens for Condit
1300 10th St.
Modesto, CA 95354

Dear Connie:

This letter is to recap something which occured here in Gary's Sacramento office and
to advise you on how to proceed according to the FPPC and the Secretary of State.

On September 12, 1988 a letter was received at the Capitol office containing a short
note indicating the letter writers approval of Gary's activities. Stapled to the
note were 5 $10 bills. Because there was no name or return address we were unable
to return the money. That day, I called the FPPC for advice on how to donate this
to charity. Cathy Donovan of the FPPC originally advised that we should treat this
as a gift in which case we had 30 days to donate it to charity. She further advised
that we should enter a notation on Gary's next Statement of Economic Interest listing
the details.

The next day she called back, however, to say that after reading the note (which I
had sent to her) she now advised that we should treat this like a campaign donation.
She added that if we wanted to donate this money to another cause, the easiest and
most effective way would be to give it to the state's general fund. On September 19,
I went to the office of the State Treasurer and gave the money to their cash management
department. They indicated a remittance slip would soon follow, which I will forward
to you.

On the advice of Ms. Donovan and Harvey Tsuboi of the Secretary of State's Political
Reform Division, we should do the following. On Gary's next Campaign Contributions
form, insert an asterisk and a note on the bottom saying that on September 12, we
received an unsolicited, anonymous donation of $50. This money was turned over to
the State's General Fund on September 19. This was never deposited in Gary's campaign
fund.

If you have any questions on this, please call me at Gary's Capitol office

Thanks,

Dan Colegrove

cc
Cathy Donovan
Harvey Tsuboi

September 20, 1988
Sacramento, CA


Ms. Constance Bird, Treasurer
Citizens for Condit
1300 10th St.
Modesto, CA 95354

Dear Connie:

This letter is to recap something which occurred here in Gary's Sacramento office and to advise you on how to proceed according to the FPPC and the Secretary of State.

On September 12, 1988 a letter was received at the Capitol office containing a short note indicating the letter writers approval of Gary's activities. Clipped to the note were 5 $10 bills. Because there was no name or return address, we were unable to return the money. That day, I called the FPPC for advice on how to donate this to charity. Cathy Donovan of the FPPC originally advised that we should treat this as a gift in which case we had 30 days to donate it to charity. She further advised that we should enter a notation on Gary's next Statement of Economic Interest listing the details.

The next day she called back, however, to say that after reading the note (which I had sent to her) she now advised that we should treat this like a campaign donation. She added that if we wanted to donate this money to another cause, the easiest and most effective way would be to give it to the state's general fund. On September 19, I went to the office of the State Treasurer and gave the money to their cash management department. They indicated a remittance slip would soon follow, which I will forward to you.

On the advice of Ms. Donovan and Harvey Tsuboi of the Secretary of State's Political Reform Division, we should do the following. On Gary's next Campaign Contributions form, insert an asterisk and a note on the bottom saying that on September 12, we received an unsolicited, anonymous donation of $50. This money was turned over to the State's General Fund on September 19. This was never deposited in Gary's campaign fund.

If you have any questions on this, please call me at Gary's Capitol office

Thanks,

Dan Colegrove

cc
Cathy Donovan
Harvey Tsuboi

September 12, 1988
Sacramento, California


Cathy Donovan
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Ms. Donovan

This is to confirm our conversation of this afternoon.  As you will recall,
I called to ask advice on how to handle a certain situation.

In Assemblyman Condits mail this morning was a small envelope with no name or
return address.  Inside was a handwritten note (enclosed) that read, "At 88 yrs.
old I'm too old to meddle much in the Government - but I like what you are doing.
Hope this helps you put your fist in Willies big mouth."  Stapled to this note were
5 $10 bills.

As we have no way of knowing who sent this, we are unable to return it.  Assemblyman
Condit decided to donate this to charity.  I called the FPPC to ask the best way
to do this and report it.  When you returned my call at approximately 4:30 pm,
you indicated that since there is nothing in the note to indicate that this is
a campaign donation, it should be treated as one treats a gift.  You told me that
a gift can be donated to charity within 30 days upon receipt.  You also advised
me to have a notation included on Assemblyman Condit's next Statement of Economic
Interest that on September 12, 1988, this money was received and to give the charity
name and the date they were sent this donation.  Finally, you told me that this
would not need to be listed on Assemblyman Condit's Consolidated Campaign Statement
to be filed with the Secretary of State.

Thank you for your advice on this situation.  If you have any further questions or
suggestions you may contact me at (916) 445-8570.

Sincerely,

DAN COLEGROVE

at 88yrs old I'm too old to middel speak in the Government - but I like it. If you are doing nigger help to feed your just use hitler big Mouth

Assemblyman Harry Cordes

Sacramento, CA
95-9
